verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (Hsp70/HSP72) is canonically a cytosolic chaperone, but it is one of the best-documented examples of cancer-cell and stress-induced ecto-display: tumor cells present Hsp70 on the outer plasma membrane leaflet (the basis for the cmHsp70.1 antibody and multiple clinical Hsp70-membrane-targeting programs), and stressed/apoptotic cells externalize Hsp70 to act as a DAMP. This is a documented cell-state-induced surface pool (cancer transformation + stress/ICD), not pMHC-only. Mechanism of outer-leaflet anchoring is non-classical (lipid binding, e.g. Bag4/PS), so it is contextual rather than baseline yes.
